input.prompt:
I'm working in a shadcn/ui component library codebase. I need to understand how existing compound components are implemented so I can create a new "Stepper" component following the same patterns.

Please explore and fully read the source of these types of components:
1. Accordion component (a compound component with context)
2. Tabs component (another compound component with context)
3. Any "Steps" or "Stepper" component if it already exists

For each component you find:
- Read the full source file
- Note how React context is used to share state between sub-components
- Note the export pattern (named exports, displayName, etc.)
- Note how variants/orientations are handled (e.g., horizontal vs vertical)
- Note how Radix UI primitives or other libraries are used (or if it's pure React)
- Note the TypeScript interface patterns

Also look for any Progress or Navigation components that might have orientation support, as those could be reference patterns.

Report back with full file paths and key implementation details.

input.description: Explore component styling and demo patterns
input.prompt:
I'm working in a shadcn/ui component library codebase. I need to understand:

1. How components are styled - do they use Tailwind CSS with cn() utility? Where is the cn() utility defined?
2. How demo/example files are structured - read 2-3 example demo files fully (look for files like *-demo.tsx or files in a demos/ folder)
3. What the typical structure of a demo file looks like (imports, component definition, exports)
4. How variants work in components - look at how cva() (class-variance-authority) is used if at all
5. Check if there's a "use client" directive pattern
6. Look at the Separator component source since the Stepper needs a separator between steps

Please read actual file contents and report:
- The path to the cn() utility
- 2-3 example demo file paths and their full contents
- The separator component path and implementation
- Any patterns around forwardRef usage
- How components handle className props

128	  light: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
129	  dark: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
130	})
131	
132	// Recursive type for CSS properties that supports empty objects at any level.
133	const cssValueSchema: z.ZodType<any> = z.lazy(() =>
134	  z.union([
135	    z.string(),
136	    z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.record(z.string(), z.string())])),
137	    z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema),

139	)
140	
141	export const registryItemCssSchema = z.record(z.string(), cssValueSchema)
142	
143	export const registryItemEnvVarsSchema = z.record(z.string(), z.string())
144	
145	// Font metadata schema for registry:font items.
146	export const registryItemFontSchema = z.object({
147	  family: z.string(),
148	  provider: z.literal("google"),
149	  import: z.string(),
150	  variable: z.string(),
151	  weight: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
152	  subsets: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
153	  selector: z.string().optional(),
154	  dependency: z.string().optional(),
155	})
156	
157	// Common fields shared by all registry items.
158	export const registryItemCommonSchema = z.object({
159	  $schema: z.string().optional(),
160	  extends: z.string().optional(),
161	  name: z.string(),
162	  title: z.string().optional(),
163	  author: z.string().min(2).optional(),
164	  description: z.string().optional(),
165	  dependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
166	  devDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
167	  registryDependencies: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
168	  files: z.array(registryItemFileSchema).optional(),
169	  tailwind: registryItemTailwindSchema.optional(),
170	  cssVars: registryItemCssVarsSchema.optional(),
171	  css: registryItemCssSchema.optional(),
172	  envVars: registryItemEnvVarsSchema.optional(),
173	  meta: z.record(z.string(), z.any()).optional(),
174	  docs: z.string().optional(),
175	  categories: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
176	})
177	
178	// registry:base has a config field, registry:font has a font field.
179	export const registryItemSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [

184	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({

186	    font: registryItemFontSchema,
187	  }),
188	  registryItemCommonSchema.extend({
189	    type: registryItemTypeSchema.exclude(["registry:base", "registry:font"]),
190	  }),
191	])

193	export type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>

197	
198	// Helper type for registry:font items specifically.
199	export type RegistryFontItem = Extract<RegistryItem, { type: "registry:font" }>
200	
201	export const registrySchema = z.object({
202	  name: z.string(),
203	  homepage: z.string(),
204	  items: z.array(registryItemSchema),
205	})
206	
207	export type Registry = z.infer<typeof registrySchema>
208	
209	export const registryIndexSchema = z.array(registryItemSchema)
210	
211	export const stylesSchema = z.array(
212	  z.object({
213	    name: z.string(),
214	    label: z.string(),
215	  })
216	)
217	
218	export const iconsSchema = z.record(
219	  z.string(),

221	)

230	  inlineColorsTemplate: z.string(),
231	  cssVarsTemplate: z.string(),
232	})
233

235	  .pick({

237	    devDependencies: true,
238	    files: true,
239	    tailwind: true,
240	    cssVars: true,
241	    css: true,
242	    envVars: true,
243	    docs: true,
244	  })
245	  .extend({

247	      .array(

252	      )
253	      .optional(),
254	  })
255	
256	export const searchResultItemSchema = z.object({

258	  type: z.string().optional(),
259	  description: z.string().optional(),

261	  addCommandArgument: z.string(),
262	})
263

267	    offset: z.number(),
268	    limit: z.number(),
269	    hasMore: z.boolean(),
270	  }),
271	  items: z.array(searchResultItemSchema),
272	})
273	
274	// Legacy schema for getRegistriesIndex() backward compatibility.

Perfect! Now let me generate a comprehensive report summarizing all the findings:

## Comprehensive Analysis: shadcn/ui Compound Component Patterns

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library. Here are the key files and implementation patterns you need to understand for building your Stepper component:

### **Key Component Files Located:**

#### 1. **Accordion Component** (Context-based Pattern)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Radix UI Primitive:** Uses `Accordion as AccordionPrimitive` from "radix-ui"
- **Export Pattern:** Named exports for each sub-component (Accordion, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent)
- **Data Slots:** Uses `data-slot="accordion"` and similar attributes for semantic identification
- **State Management:** Delegates all state to Radix UI primitive - no custom context needed
- **Styling:** Uses Tailwind classes with CVA-ready approach, cn() utility for merging
- **Animation:** Uses data attributes like `data-[state=closed]:animate-accordion-up`

#### 2. **Tabs Component** (Orientation + Variant Support)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/tabs.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Radix UI Primitive:** Uses `Tabs as TabsPrimitive` from "radix-ui"
- **Orientation Support:** `orientation = "horizontal"` prop with `data-orientation` attribute
- **CVA Variants:** Implements variant patterns with `class-variance-authority` for TabsList (default vs line)
- **Data Attributes:** Uses both `data-slot` and `data-variant`, `data-orientation` for styling hooks
- **Export Pattern:** Exports main component + sub-components + variant helper (`tabsListVariants`)
- **Group Selectors:** Uses Tailwind group selectors like `group/tabs` and `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:` for sibling-aware styling
- **State Handling:** Delegates to Radix with `data-[state=active]` and `data-[state=open]` selectors

#### 3. **Navigation Menu Component** (Complex Compound)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/navigation-menu.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Radix UI Primitive:** Uses NavigationMenu primitives
- **CVA Helper:** Exports `navigationMenuTriggerStyle` as a CVA function for reuse
- **Optional Children:** Conditionally renders NavigationMenuViewport based on prop
- **Comprehensive Sub-components:** Multiple sub-components for complex structure (Trigger, Content, Viewport, Indicator, Link)
- **Data Slots:** Extensive use of `data-slot` naming convention

#### 4. **Sidebar Component** (Advanced Context Pattern - Best Reference!)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/sidebar.tsx`

**Key Patterns - BEST REFERENCE FOR CONTEXT USAGE:**
- **React Context Setup:**
  ```typescript
  type SidebarContextProps = { state, open, setOpen, openMobile, setOpenMobile, isMobile, toggleSidebar }
  const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)
  function useSidebar() { 
    const context = React.useContext(SidebarContext)
    if (!context) throw new Error("useSidebar must be used within...")
    return context
  }
  ```
- **Provider Pattern:** SidebarProvider wraps children and provides context
- **State Management:** Handles both controlled and uncontrolled states (`open` vs `openProp`)
- **useMemo for Context:** Memoizes context value to prevent unnecessary re-renders
- **Hooks Integration:** Uses custom hook `useIsMobile()` from `@/registry/new-york-v4/hooks/use-mobile`
- **Variant Props:** Multiple variants (`side`, `variant`, `collapsible`)
- **Data Attributes:** Extensive use of `data-state`, `data-variant`, `data-side`, `data-collapsible`
- **CSS Variables:** Uses CSS custom properties like `--sidebar-width`, `--sidebar-width-icon`
- **Composition:** 18 exported functions showing deep hierarchy structure

#### 5. **Progress Component** (Simple Radix Wrapper)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/progress.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Minimal Wrapper:** Simple wrapper around ProgressPrimitive.Root
- **Data Slot:** Uses `data-slot="progress"`
- **No Variants:** Straightforward single export
- **Inline Styling:** Uses `style` prop for dynamic values

#### 6. **Button Group Component** (Orientation Variant Pattern)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button-group.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **CVA with Orientation:** Excellent example of orientation-based variants
- **asChild Pattern:** Uses `Slot.Root` from radix-ui for polymorphic components
- **Data Attributes:** `data-slot` and `data-orientation`
- **Multiple Exports:** Component + separator + text + variants helper

#### 7. **Breadcrumb Component** (Semantic Compound)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/breadcrumb.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **asChild Pattern:** Uses `Slot.Root` for polymorphic rendering
- **Semantic HTML:** Proper nav/ol/li structure
- **ARIA Attributes:** `aria-label`, `aria-current`, `role` attributes
- **Default Children:** BreadcrumbSeparator has default ChevronRight icon

#### 8. **Table Component** (Pure Semantic HTML)
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/table.tsx`

**Key Patterns:**
- **Wrapper Pattern:** Table wraps with a container div for scroll handling
- **Data Slots:** All sub-elements have `data-slot` attributes
- **Pure HTML:** Maps directly to table/thead/tbody/etc.

### **Cross-Cutting Patterns for Your Stepper Component:**

1. **Export Structure:**
   - Main component function
   - Sub-component functions (Step, StepTrigger, StepContent, etc.)
   - Variant helpers exported separately if using CVA

2. **Data Attributes:**
   - Always use `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-slot="stepper-step"`, etc.
   - Use `data-orientation` for horizontal/vertical support
   - Use `data-state="active"` or `data-index` for current step tracking
   - Use `data-variant` if supporting multiple visual styles

3. **Context Approach (Recommended for Stepper):**
   - Create `StepperContext` for sharing state between steps
   - Implement `useStepper()` hook with error if not in provider
   - Provide current step index, total steps, orientation
   - Store in `useMemo` to prevent re-renders

4. **Orientation Support:**
   - Follow the Tabs/ButtonGroup pattern with `orientation` prop
   - Use group selectors: `group/stepper` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:`
   - Adjust flex direction based on orientation

5. **Styling Tools:**
   - Import `cn` from `/lib/utils` (uses clsx + tailwind-merge)
   - Use CVA from `class-variance-authority` for complex variants
   - Use `Slot` from radix-ui for `asChild` polymorphism

6. **TypeScript Patterns:**
   ```typescript
   React.ComponentProps<typeof SomeElement> // For spreading native props
   React.ComponentProps<typeof RadixPrimitive.Root> // For Radix integration
   VariantProps<typeof cvaMachine> // For variant props
   ```

7. **Display Names:**
   - Each component function implicitly has a display name from its function name
   - No need to explicitly set displayName

### **No Existing Stepper Component:**
Confirmed - there is no existing stepper component in the codebase, so you have full freedom to design following these established patterns.

### **Recommended Stepper Architecture:**

Based on the Sidebar (best reference) and Tabs (orientation example) patterns:

```
Stepper (Provider component with context)
├── StepperContent (wrapper for visual container)
├── Step (individual step)
│   ├── StepTrigger (clickable/interactive step header)
│   └── StepContent (step body content)
└── useStepper() hook for child components
```

**Use the Sidebar as your primary reference** for context implementation, and **use Tabs as reference for orientation support**.

#### 4. **Sidebar Component** (Advanced Context Pattern - Best Reference!)

  ```typescript

  const SidebarContext = React.createContext<SidebarContextProps | null>(null)
  ```

### **Cross-Cutting Patterns for Your Stepper Component:**

1. **Export Structure:**
   - Main component function
   - Sub-component functions (Step, StepTrigger, StepContent, etc.)
   - Variant helpers exported separately if using CVA

2. **Data Attributes:**
   - Always use `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-slot="stepper-step"`, etc.
   - Use `data-orientation` for horizontal/vertical support
   - Use `data-state="active"` or `data-index` for current step tracking
   - Use `data-variant` if supporting multiple visual styles

3. **Context Approach (Recommended for Stepper):**
   - Create `StepperContext` for sharing state between steps
   - Implement `useStepper()` hook with error if not in provider
   - Provide current step index, total steps, orientation
   - Store in `useMemo` to prevent re-renders

4. **Orientation Support:**
   - Follow the Tabs/ButtonGroup pattern with `orientation` prop
   - Use group selectors: `group/stepper` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:`
   - Adjust flex direction based on orientation

5. **Styling Tools:**
   - Import `cn` from `/lib/utils` (uses clsx + tailwind-merge)
   - Use CVA from `class-variance-authority` for complex variants
   - Use `Slot` from radix-ui for `asChild` polymorphism

6. **TypeScript Patterns:**
   ```typescript
   React.ComponentProps<typeof SomeElement> // For spreading native props
   React.ComponentProps<typeof RadixPrimitive.Root> // For Radix integration
   VariantProps<typeof cvaMachine> // For variant props
   ```

7. **Display Names:**
   - Each component function implicitly has a display name from its function name
   - No need to explicitly set displayName

### **No Existing Stepper Component:**
Confirmed - there is no existing stepper component in the codebase, so you have full freedom to design following these established patterns.

### **Recommended Stepper Architecture:**

Based on the Sidebar (best reference) and Tabs (orientation example) patterns:

```
Stepper (Provider component with context)
├── StepperContent (wrapper for visual container)
├── Step (individual step)
│   ├── StepTrigger (clickable/interactive step header)
│   └── StepContent (step body content)
└── useStepper() hook for child components
```

**Use the Sidebar as your primary reference** for context implementation, and **use Tabs as reference for orientation support**.

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings:

## Analysis Report: shadcn/ui Component Library Patterns

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase, here are the key findings:

### 1. **How Components Are Styled - cn() Utility**

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`

The `cn()` utility is implemented as:

```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

**How it works:**
- Uses `clsx` to merge class names conditionally
- Uses `twMerge` from `tailwind-merge` to intelligently merge Tailwind CSS classes (resolves conflicts)
- This pattern allows props-based className merging while preventing conflicting Tailwind utilities

### 2. **Demo File Structure**

**Example Demo File Paths:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/separator-demo.tsx`
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/button-demo.tsx`
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/card-demo.tsx`

**Separator Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive (it's a simple demo)
- Imports the component from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator`
- Exports a default function component
- Uses the component with props like `className="my-4"` and `orientation="vertical"`
- Purely presentational with no state management

**Button Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports the Button component
- Demonstrates different variants and sizes
- Uses icon imports from `lucide-react`
- Minimal - shows basic usage patterns

**Card Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports multiple Card sub-components (CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, etc.)
- Shows composition pattern with nested card components
- Includes form elements and buttons
- Demonstrates realistic usage in a login form context

**Accordion Demo Structure (additional example):**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports related sub-components (AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent)
- Default export function with descriptive name
- Uses component-specific props (type="single", collapsible, defaultValue)
- Rich content with text blocks

### 3. **Typical Demo File Pattern**

```typescript
import { ComponentName } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component-name"

export default function ComponentNameDemo() {
  return (
    // JSX content demonstrating the component
  )
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Named exports as `[ComponentName]Demo`
- Imports from the registry UI folder
- No hooks or state (pure demo)
- No "use client" directive (these are pre-rendered)
- Focused, concise examples

### 4. **Variants with cva() (Class Variance Authority)**

**Example from Button component** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button.tsx`):

```typescript
import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"

const buttonVariants = cva(
  "base classes here",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "default variant classes",
        destructive: "destructive variant classes",
        outline: "outline variant classes",
        // ... more variants
      },
      size: {
        default: "default size classes",
        xs: "extra small classes",
        sm: "small classes",
        // ... more sizes
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
      size: "default",
    },
  }
)

function Button({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> & VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants>) {
  // Component uses: cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))
}
```

**Pattern:** CVA is used for all styled components to manage variant combinations with type safety.

### 5. **"use client" Directive Pattern**

The `"use client"` directive is used **selectively** for components that use Radix UI primitives or other client-side functionality:

**Components WITH "use client":**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/checkbox.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/label.tsx`

**Components WITHOUT "use client":**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/alert.tsx`

**Rule:** Use "use client" when the component wraps Radix primitives or needs interactivity; omit it for simple styled wrapper components.

### 6. **Separator Component Implementation**

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`

```typescript
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import { Separator as SeparatorPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

function Separator({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  decorative = true,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SeparatorPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <SeparatorPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="separator"
      decorative={decorative}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn(
        "shrink-0 bg-border data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full data-[orientation=vertical]:h-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px",
        className
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

export { Separator }
```

**Key features:**
- Wraps Radix UI Separator primitive
- Uses `data-[orientation]` selectors for conditional styling
- Supports both horizontal and vertical orientations
- Default props: `orientation="horizontal"`, `decorative={true}`
- Uses `data-slot="separator"` for styling hooks
- Merges className with `cn()` utility

### 7. **forwardRef Usage Pattern**

**Finding:** Components in this library **do NOT use `forwardRef`** directly. Instead, they:
- Wrap Radix UI primitives which handle ref forwarding internally via their Root components
- Use `React.ComponentProps<typeof PrimitiveRoot>` to extract and preserve all component props including refs
- The pattern relies on the underlying primitive to handle refs automatically

**Example:** The Separator, Checkbox, and Label components all wrap Radix primitives that handle refs internally through their `.Root` components.

### 8. **How Components Handle className Props**

**Pattern observed across all components:**

1. **Extract className from props:**
   ```typescript
   function Component({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   ```

2. **Merge with cn() utility:**
   ```typescript
   className={cn("base-styles", className)}
   ```

3. **Spread remaining props:**
   ```typescript
   {...props}
   ```

4. **Always include data-slot attribute:**
   ```typescript
   data-slot="component-name"
   ```

This pattern ensures:
- Base styles are applied first
- User-provided className can override via Tailwind's merge logic
- All other HTML attributes are preserved
- Consistent debugging via data-slot attributes

---

## Summary for Stepper Implementation

For your Stepper component, you should:
1. Use `cn()` from `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`
2. Use `cva()` for variant management
3. Include `"use client"` if wrapping Radix primitives
4. Use the Separator component at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx` between steps
5. Follow the composition pattern with sub-components (Step, StepTrigger, StepContent)
6. Use `data-slot` attributes for styling and debugging

The `cn()` utility is implemented as:

```typescript
import { clsx, type ClassValue } from "clsx"
import { twMerge } from "tailwind-merge"

export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) {
  return twMerge(clsx(inputs))
}
```

**How it works:**
- Uses `clsx` to merge class names conditionally
- Uses `twMerge` from `tailwind-merge` to intelligently merge Tailwind CSS classes (resolves conflicts)
- This pattern allows props-based className merging while preventing conflicting Tailwind utilities

### 2. **Demo File Structure**

**Example Demo File Paths:**
1. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/separator-demo.tsx`
2. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/button-demo.tsx`
3. `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/card-demo.tsx`

**Separator Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive (it's a simple demo)
- Imports the component from `@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator`
- Exports a default function component
- Uses the component with props like `className="my-4"` and `orientation="vertical"`
- Purely presentational with no state management

**Button Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports the Button component
- Demonstrates different variants and sizes
- Uses icon imports from `lucide-react`
- Minimal - shows basic usage patterns

**Card Demo Structure:**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports multiple Card sub-components (CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, etc.)
- Shows composition pattern with nested card components
- Includes form elements and buttons
- Demonstrates realistic usage in a login form context

**Accordion Demo Structure (additional example):**
- No `"use client"` directive
- Imports related sub-components (AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger, AccordionContent)
- Default export function with descriptive name
- Uses component-specific props (type="single", collapsible, defaultValue)
- Rich content with text blocks

### 3. **Typical Demo File Pattern**

```typescript
import { ComponentName } from "@/registry/new-york-v4/ui/component-name"

export default function ComponentNameDemo() {
  return (
    // JSX content demonstrating the component
  )
}
```

**Key characteristics:**
- Named exports as `[ComponentName]Demo`
- Imports from the registry UI folder
- No hooks or state (pure demo)
- No "use client" directive (these are pre-rendered)
- Focused, concise examples

### 4. **Variants with cva() (Class Variance Authority)**

**Example from Button component** (`/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/button.tsx`):

```typescript
import { cva, type VariantProps } from "class-variance-authority"

const buttonVariants = cva(
  "base classes here",
  {
    variants: {
      variant: {
        default: "default variant classes",
        destructive: "destructive variant classes",
        outline: "outline variant classes",
        // ... more variants
      },
      size: {
        default: "default size classes",
        xs: "extra small classes",
        sm: "small classes",
        // ... more sizes
      },
    },
    defaultVariants: {
      variant: "default",
      size: "default",
    },
  }
)

function Button({
  className,
  variant = "default",
  size = "default",
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<"button"> & VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants>) {
  // Component uses: cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size, className }))
}
```

**Pattern:** CVA is used for all styled components to manage variant combinations with type safety.

### 5. **"use client" Directive Pattern**

The `"use client"` directive is used **selectively** for components that use Radix UI primitives or other client-side functionality:

**Components WITH "use client":**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/checkbox.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/label.tsx`

**Components WITHOUT "use client":**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/card.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/input.tsx`
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/alert.tsx`

**Rule:** Use "use client" when the component wraps Radix primitives or needs interactivity; omit it for simple styled wrapper components.

### 6. **Separator Component Implementation**

**Path:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx`

```typescript
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import { Separator as SeparatorPrimitive } from "radix-ui"
import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

function Separator({
  className,
  orientation = "horizontal",
  decorative = true,
  ...props
}: React.ComponentProps<typeof SeparatorPrimitive.Root>) {
  return (
    <SeparatorPrimitive.Root
      data-slot="separator"
      decorative={decorative}
      orientation={orientation}
      className={cn(
        "shrink-0 bg-border data-[orientation=horizontal]:h-px data-[orientation=horizontal]:w-full data-[orientation=vertical]:h-full data-[orientation=vertical]:w-px",
        className
      )}
      {...props}
    />
  )
}

export { Separator }
```

**Key features:**
- Wraps Radix UI Separator primitive
- Uses `data-[orientation]` selectors for conditional styling
- Supports both horizontal and vertical orientations
- Default props: `orientation="horizontal"`, `decorative={true}`
- Uses `data-slot="separator"` for styling hooks
- Merges className with `cn()` utility

### 7. **forwardRef Usage Pattern**

**Finding:** Components in this library **do NOT use `forwardRef`** directly. Instead, they:
- Wrap Radix UI primitives which handle ref forwarding internally via their Root components
- Use `React.ComponentProps<typeof PrimitiveRoot>` to extract and preserve all component props including refs
- The pattern relies on the underlying primitive to handle refs automatically

**Example:** The Separator, Checkbox, and Label components all wrap Radix primitives that handle refs internally through their `.Root` components.

### 8. **How Components Handle className Props**

**Pattern observed across all components:**

1. **Extract className from props:**
   ```typescript
   function Component({ className, ...props }: React.ComponentProps<"div">) {
   ```

2. **Merge with cn() utility:**
   ```typescript
   className={cn("base-styles", className)}
   ```

3. **Spread remaining props:**
   ```typescript
   {...props}
   ```

4. **Always include data-slot attribute:**
   ```typescript
   data-slot="component-name"
   ```

This pattern ensures:
- Base styles are applied first
- User-provided className can override via Tailwind's merge logic
- All other HTML attributes are preserved
- Consistent debugging via data-slot attributes

---

## Summary for Stepper Implementation

For your Stepper component, you should:
1. Use `cn()` from `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/lib/utils.ts`
2. Use `cva()` for variant management
3. Include `"use client"` if wrapping Radix primitives
4. Use the Separator component at `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/separator.tsx` between steps
5. Follow the composition pattern with sub-components (Step, StepTrigger, StepContent)
6. Use `data-slot` attributes for styling and debugging

Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive report:

## Shadcn/UI Component Library - Project Structure Report

I've thoroughly explored the shadcn/ui component library project and gathered detailed information about its registry system and component structure. Here's my complete analysis:

---

### 1. TOP-LEVEL DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Root Directory:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775474037751-retn7b/`

**Key Structure:**
- **`/apps/v4/`** - Main Next.js application (the demo site)
- **`/packages/shadcn/`** - Core shadcn CLI package
- **`/packages/tests/`** - Test packages
- **`/templates/`** - Template files
- **`/scripts/`** - Root-level scripts

**Key Configuration Files:**
- `package.json` - Monorepo configuration with pnpm workspaces
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` - Workspace definition
- `tsconfig.json` - Root TypeScript config
- `turbo.json` - Turbo build orchestration config

---

### 2. REGISTRY SYSTEM - HOW IT WORKS

The registry system is a sophisticated, multi-layered system with both TypeScript definitions and JSON outputs:

#### **2A. Registry Schema (TypeScript Definitions)**

**File:** `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`

This is the authoritative source for registry types. Key schemas include:

```typescript
// Main registry type enum
registryItemTypeSchema = z.enum([
  "registry:lib",
  "registry:block",
  "registry:component",
  "registry:ui",
  "registry:hook",
  "registry:page",
  "registry:file",
  "registry:theme",
  "registry:style",
  "registry:item",
  "registry:base",
  "registry:font",
  "registry:example",      // Internal use only
  "registry:internal",      // Internal use only
])
```

**Core Registry Item Structure:**
```typescript
{
  $schema?: string
  extends?: string
  name: string                          // Required: unique component identifier
  type: registryItemType                // Required: item category
  title?: string                        // Optional: display title
  description?: string                  // Optional: description
  dependencies?: string[]               // NPM packages required
  devDependencies?: string[]            // Dev NPM packages
  registryDependencies?: string[]       // Other registry items required
  files: registryItemFile[]             // Array of source files
  tailwind?: TailwindConfig             // Optional: Tailwind CSS config
  cssVars?: CSSVarsSchema               // Optional: CSS variable definitions
  css?: CSSSchema                       // Optional: raw CSS rules
  envVars?: Record<string, string>      // Optional: environment variables
  meta?: Record<string, any>            // Optional: arbitrary metadata
  docs?: string                         // Optional: documentation link
  categories?: string[]                 // Optional: categorization
}
```

**File Object Schema:**
```typescript
{
  path: string                          // Relative path to source file
  content?: string                      // Optional: inline file content
  type: registryItemType               // File type (must match item type or be registry:component)
  target?: string                       // Required for registry:file/page; output location
}
```

---

#### **2B. TypeScript Registry Definitions**

**Main Registry Building Blocks:**

1. **UI Components** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`
   - 108 UI components (buttons, cards, dialogs, etc.)
   - Standard component format
   
2. **Examples** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`
   - 476 example/demo files
   - Showcasing component variants and usage
   
3. **Blocks** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/blocks/_registry.ts`
   - 166 pre-built blocks (dashboards, sidebars, login pages)
   - Complex multi-component layouts
   
4. **Charts** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/charts/_registry.ts`
   - Chart component examples
   - Categorized by type (area, bar, pie, etc.)
   
5. **Hooks** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/hooks/_registry.ts`
   - Custom React hooks (e.g., `use-mobile`)
   - Type: `registry:hook`
   
6. **Lib** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/lib/_registry.ts`
   - Utility libraries (e.g., `utils`)
   - Type: `registry:lib`
   
7. **Internal** - `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/internal/_registry.ts`
   - Internal components used by blocks
   - Type: `registry:internal`

---

#### **2C. Master Registry Assembly**

**File:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/registry.ts`

This is the master registry that combines all sub-registries:

```typescript
export const registry = {
  name: "shadcn/ui",
  homepage: "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  items: z.array(registryItemSchema).parse([
    // Shared style definitions
    { name: "index", type: "registry:style", ... },
    { name: "style", type: "registry:style", ... },
    // All sub-registries merged together
    ...ui,
    ...blocks,
    ...charts,
    ...lib,
    ...hooks,
    ...themes,
    ...examples,
    ...internal,
  ].filter((item) => {
    return !DEPRECATED_ITEMS.includes(item.name)
  })
}
```

---

#### **2D. Generated JSON Registries**

**File:** `/apps/v4/registry.json` (153,713 bytes)

This is the compiled JSON output containing all registry items with full metadata. Generated by the build system.

**Sample Structure:**
```json
{
  "name": "shadcn/ui",
  "homepage": "https://ui.shadcn.com",
  "items": [
    {
      "name": "accordion",
      "type": "registry:ui",
      "dependencies": ["@radix-ui/react-accordion"],
      "files": [{
        "path": "registry/new-york-v4/ui/accordion.tsx",
        "type": "registry:ui"
      }]
    },
    // ... 750+ more items
  ]
}
```

---

### 3. COMPONENT FILE LOCATIONS

**Base Location:** `/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/`

#### **3A. UI Components**
- **Path:** `ui/` (59 component files)
- **Format:** `{component-name}.tsx`
- **Examples:**
  - `ui/accordion.tsx`
  - `ui/button.tsx`
  - `ui/card.tsx`
  - `ui/dialog.tsx`
  - `ui/form.tsx`
  - `ui/sidebar.tsx`
  - etc.

#### **3B. Example/Demo Files**

Actually, I have enough to plan. Let me now design the Stepper component and create a comprehensive plan.


The Stepper needs a root container component with context to manage state, along with sub-components for the list, individual items, triggers, labels, descriptions, separators, and content panels. I'll structure it with props for controlled and uncontrolled modes, orientation support, and step change callbacks. Setting up the context structure with a main stepper context to manage the current step and orientation, plus an item context to pass step numbers down to child components like triggers and titles.

Defining the component hierarchy: a root Stepper provider, a StepperList container for navigation, individual StepperItem wrappers for each step, and then the trigger, title, description, separator, and panel subcomponents that each consume the context to apply the right attributes and styling.

Now I'm thinking through the styling approach—horizontal orientation should stack the step list at the top with the panel content below, while vertical puts the steps along the left side with the panel on the right. I'll use flexbox with orientation-aware data attributes to handle the layout direction, and each component like the trigger and separator will have their own flex utilities for alignment and spacing. Building out the component structure... Writing component styles... Building the component structure... I'm working through the layout structure for a horizontal stepper component. The key insight is that each step should display the indicator circle alongside the title, with a flexible separator line stretching between steps to create that connected visual flow.

For the vertical variant, I'm adjusting the separator to be a short vertical line positioned between steps instead. Now I'm ready to implement this as a self-contained React component with context, so I'll start building out the actual stepper file.

Writing stepper component... Writing the Stepper component... Writing the StepperItem component...

Now I'm building the StepperTrigger button that lets users navigate between steps—it tracks the current step state and applies focus styles, disabled states, and click handlers to update which step is active.

Building component styles... Finishing the StepperPanel component...

Actually, I'm realizing there's a potential issue with how StepperList accesses the orientation context—it should work fine since it's rendered as a child of the Stepper provider, but I need to make sure the context is properly available at that point in the component tree.

For the layout structure, I'm thinking through how StepperItem and StepperSeparator should nest together. In a horizontal orientation, the StepperList acts as a flex-row container, with StepperItems and StepperSeparators alternating—the separators should flex-grow to fill the space between items, so they need to live inside the StepperList as siblings to the items.

In vertical mode, the same alternating pattern applies, but the separator becomes a vertical line with left margin to align under the step indicator.

Now I'm reconsidering whether I need a dedicated StepperIndicator component. The user didn't explicitly ask for one, but a step indicator—the circle with a number or checkmark—is pretty essential to a stepper UI, so I should probably include it as a sub-component.

Building the indicator component...

Now I need to think through how `StepperSeparator` should work—it needs access to the orientation from the parent stepper context, but it's being rendered inside `StepperList`, so I should make sure it can properly access that information. I'm checking the import paths for the `cn` utility across different components. The UI components like accordion and tabs use `@/lib/utils`, while the sidebar uses the registry-specific path since it's a more complex component that might need different utilities.

For the Stepper component, I'll stick with `@/lib/utils` to match the pattern. For the demo, I need to decide between an uncontrolled stepper with just click-to-navigate functionality or one with Next/Previous buttons that require state management. I'm leaning toward keeping it simple with an uncontrolled stepper where clicking steps navigates directly.

Since the Stepper itself is a `"use client"` component handling all its own state, the demo file doesn't actually need the directive—the client boundary is already established at the component level, similar to how other demos work with Radix-based components.

For the demo, I'll skip the Next/Previous buttons and let users interact directly with the step triggers instead. I'm setting up the demo structure with the Stepper component imported and a steps array defining the flow through account creation, email verification, and profile setup.

Building the demo component... Building the stepper component structure...

Now I'm thinking about how to set up the registry entries for this component.

I'm defining the registry entry for the stepper UI component, specifying the file path and type. Since the component only relies on React and the existing utility functions, I don't need to add any external dependencies like Radix UI or class-variance-authority.

# Plan: Add Stepper Compound Component to shadcn/ui ## Context The project is a shadcn/ui monorepo (`apps/v4` is the main Next.js app). The goal is to add a new `Stepper` compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows — with sub-components, React context state sharing, horizontal/vertical orientation, a demo, and registry entries. No existing files should be modified except to append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create 1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — Component implementation 2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — Demo example ## Files to Modify (append entries only) 3. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** — Add `stepper` entry between `"spinner"` (ends ~line 563) and `"switch"` (~line 564) 4. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`** — Add `stepper-demo` entry between `"spinner-item"` (~line 2124) and `"switch-demo"` (~line 2125) --- ## Key Patterns to Follow (from codebase analysis) - **`cn()` utility**: import from `@/lib/utils` (clsx + tailwind-merge) - **`"use client"`**: required since the component uses `React.useState` / `React.createContext` - **Data attributes**: `data-slot`, `data-orientation`, `data-state`, `data-step` on all elements - **Group selectors**: `group/stepper` on root + `group-data-[orientation=...]/stepper:` in children (see `tabs.tsx` pattern) - **No Radix UI dependency**: pure React context — no external NPM dep needed beyond `lucide-react` (already implicit, like `accordion.tsx`) - **ComponentProps pattern**: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` spread for all sub-components - **Named exports only**: no default export, no `displayName` needed (inferred from function name) --- ## Step 1 — Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Architecture Two React contexts: - **`StepperContext`** (root) — shares `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation` - **`StepperItemContext`** (per item) — shares `step`, `isActive`, `isCompleted` Two exported hooks: - **`useStepper()`** — throws if used outside `Stepper` - (internal) `useStepperItem()` — used by sub-components, not exported ### Sub-components | Component | Element | data-slot | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `Stepper` | `
` | `stepper` | Context provider; `group/stepper`; supports `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `orientation` | | `StepperList` | `
` | `stepper-list` | `flex-row` (h) / `flex-col items-start` (v) via group selectors | | `StepperItem` | `
` | `stepper-item` | `StepperItemContext` provider; `data-state="active|completed|inactive"` | | `StepperTrigger` | `

The goal is to add a new `Stepper` compound component for multi-step wizards and onboarding flows — with sub-components, React context state sharing, horizontal/vertical orientation, a demo, and registry entries. No existing files should be modified except to append registry entries. --- ## Files to Create 1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — Component implementation 2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — Demo example ## Files to Modify (append entries only) 3. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`** — Add `stepper` entry between `"spinner"` (ends ~line 563) and `"switch"` (~line 564) 4. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`** — Add `stepper-demo` entry between `"spinner-item"` (~line 2124) and `"switch-demo"` (~line 2125) --- ## Key Patterns to Follow (from codebase analysis) - **`cn()` utility**: import from `@/lib/utils` (clsx + tailwind-merge) - **`"use client"`**: required since the component uses `React.useState` / `React.createContext` - **Data attributes**: `data-slot`, `data-orientation`, `data-state`, `data-step` on all elements - **Group selectors**: `group/stepper` on root + `group-data-[orientation=...]/stepper:` in children (see `tabs.tsx` pattern) - **No Radix UI dependency**: pure React context — no external NPM dep needed beyond `lucide-react` (already implicit, like `accordion.tsx`) - **ComponentProps pattern**: `React.ComponentProps<"div">` spread for all sub-components - **Named exports only**: no default export, no `displayName` needed (inferred from function name) --- ## Step 1 — Create `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` ### Architecture Two React contexts: - **`StepperContext`** (root) — shares `value`, `onValueChange`, `orientation` - **`StepperItemContext`** (per item) — shares `step`, `isActive`, `isCompleted` Two exported hooks: - **`useStepper()`** — throws if used outside `Stepper` - (internal) `useStepperItem()` — used by sub-components, not exported ### Sub-components | Component | Element | data-slot | Notes | |---|---|---|---| | `Stepper` | `
` | `stepper` | Context provider; `group/stepper`; supports `value`, `defaultValue`, `onValueChange`, `orientation` | | `StepperList` | `
` | `stepper-list` | `flex-row` (h) / `flex-col items-start` (v) via group selectors | | `StepperItem` | `
` | `stepper-item` | `StepperItemContext` provider; `data-state="active|completed|inactive"` | | `StepperTrigger` | `

Building the stepper component with two context layers: a root `StepperContext` for managing the overall state and orientation, and a `StepperItemContext` for tracking individual step states, then exporting the main `Stepper` component along with nine sub-components and a hook for accessing the context.

Building stepper component... Writing the component structure... Finishing the callback setup and wrapping everything in the context provider, then building out the StepperList component with responsive flex layout based on orientation...

Defining the StepperItem component to track which step is active and whether it's been completed, using the context to determine its state... Writing component styles... Writing the stepper components...

Actually, I'm noticing a potential issue with how `StepperIndicator` handles the `children` prop—if we're using `props.children ?? (...)` but `children` is already spread into `...props`, we might end up rendering children twice. I need to destructure `children` separately and handle it more carefully.

Looking at the structure, the `StepperIndicator` should render the passed children if provided, otherwise fall back to showing either a checkmark icon when the step is completed or the step number itself. The component also needs to properly apply the active and completed states through className styling.

For the root `Stepper` component, I'm thinking about how to handle orientation—using a CSS group selector with `group/stepper` so that child components can respond to the orientation state via Tailwind group selectors, with the orientation data attribute driving the styling for `StepperList` and `StepperSeparator`. I'm getting caught up on whether the Stepper root should always be `flex-col` or if it should change based on orientation. Looking back at the plan, the root container uses `group/stepper` and `data-orientation` attributes to control how children style themselves via group selectors, so the root probably just stays `flex-col` consistently. For the demo, I'll focus on building out a horizontal wizard layout first to keep things concrete. I'm working out the group selector syntax for handling orientation-based layout changes, then moving on to the separator styling—horizontal needs a thin line with flex growth, while vertical requires a line that aligns with the step indicator's center, which means calculating the proper left margin offset.

Now I'm structuring the component implementation, starting with how `StepperIndicator` should accept and destructure its children prop, then thinking through the layout differences between horizontal and vertical modes—horizontal stacks items with separators between them, while vertical arranges them in a column where each item shows the indicator alongside its text content with separators below. I'm settling on a cleaner structure where `StepperItem` uses flex-row for vertical layouts with the indicator and text side-by-side, while horizontal layouts stack them vertically. The `StepperTrigger` will be a button that contains both the indicator and text content, keeping the component API simple and flexible. I'm settling on a structure where the stepper items and separators are siblings within a list, with the content panels separate below—this keeps the markup clean and the visual hierarchy clear. So the separator needs to use group data selectors to respond to the parent Stepper's orientation, applying different dimensions depending on whether it's horizontal or vertical. Now I'm writing out the full component with proper children handling in the indicator, and setting up a demo with a three-step wizard that includes navigation buttons and content panels to showcase how everything works together. uncontrolled pattern vs controlled `value` + `onValueChange`.

OK I think I have everything I need. Let me write the files now.

Actually, one more thing to check - the `StepperIndicator` children issue. In React, when you spread `...props`, it doesn't include `children` because `children` is part of `React.ComponentProps`. Let me destructure `children` explicitly so I can use it with a fallback to default content.

Also, for the `StepperItem`, when rendering children that use `useStepperItem()`, they need to be within the `StepperItemContext.Provider`. That's fine since it's a context provider wrapping the children.

Now I'm looking at the `StepperSeparator` styling - it needs to use group selectors that reference the root `Stepper` div's orientation, similar to how the tabs component uses `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/tabs:` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/tabs:`. I'll apply the same pattern here with `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/stepper:` and `group-data-[orientation=vertical]/stepper:`.

For the `StepperItem`, I need to expose the `data-state` attribute as "active", "completed", or "inactive". Since it takes a `step` prop but also accepts an optional `completed` override, I can use that to determine the state. Now I'm working through how `StepperList` should handle flex layout based on orientation—horizontal uses flex-row with centered items, while vertical uses flex-col with items aligned to the start. For `StepperItem` itself, I need to consider that in horizontal mode the indicator and title sit side by side, but the layout gets more complex when thinking about how separators and vertical arrangements interact. I'm clarifying the component hierarchy—`StepperList` contains alternating `StepperItem` and `StepperSeparator` elements, where each item wraps the trigger with its indicator and title, and the separators stretch between them horizontally.

For the vertical layout, I'm adjusting the structure so the separator becomes a vertical line with left margin to align under the indicator, and the title and description sit together in a wrapper. Now I'm ready to start implementing the component.

Writing stepper component... Writing the Stepper component... Writing the stepper components... Writing component styles...

Now I have enough context to implement the Stepper component. Let me plan out the implementation:

1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`**: A compound component with React contexts
2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`**: Demo with 3 steps

4. Modify `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts` - add stepper-demo between spinner-item and switch-demo

Now I'll build the stepper component using the same patterns as tabs.tsx - it'll be a client-side component with React contexts for managing state, using group selectors for orientation, and importing CheckIcon from lucide-react instead of relying on Radix UI.

Writing stepper component... Writing the Stepper component... Writing component structure... Writing component styles... Writing component styles... Building the demo component... Writing the stepper panels and navigation...

Now I'm thinking through how the separator should be positioned in both horizontal and vertical layouts. In a horizontal stepper, the separator needs to sit between the step indicators at the same vertical level, while in vertical mode it should be a line connecting them vertically. The key is getting the alignment right so the separator visually connects the steps properly.

I'm realizing the pattern from tabs.tsx applies here too—I should put the `group/stepper` class on the root `Stepper` component and use `group-data-[orientation=horizontal]/stepper:` selectors to apply different styles based on the orientation attribute.

Now I'm ready to write out the implementation, starting with the demo file that needs the `"use client"` directive since it manages React state for the wizard navigation.
